column3: Cell Cultured Meat: Chronoamperometry for Lactate Conversion

column3: Cell Cultured Meat: Cyclic Voltammetry for Lactate Detection

column3: Cell Cultured Meat: Lactate Quantification using L-Lactate Assay
column4: Standard calibration curve constructed using the protocol. Initial and Final absorbance measured as per protocol. Actual sample absorbance and concentration calculated.
column18: Cell Cultured Meat, cellular agriculture, omics approaches, bioreactors, greenhouse gas mitigation

column3: Cell Cultured Meat: Lactate Quantification using Spectrophotometry
column4: Standard calibration curve of L-Lactic acid is constructed. Absorbance of Blank wells are initially taken and subtracted from Absorbance values obtained by adding samples at that particular wavelength. Reference is considered as the absorbance of sample with 0 concentration of L-Lactic acid and is subtracted from sample absorbance to get the actual absorbance. Based on the obtained values of actual absorbance, the concentration of L-Lactic acid is determined in samples from the calibration curve.
column18: Cell Cultured Meat, cellular agriculture, omics approaches, bioreactors, greenhouse gas mitigation

column4: This schema is designed for environmental or agricultural monitoring datasets. It includes several key attributes such as sample identifiers, sample types, geographic coordinates (latitude and longitude), and site descriptions. The schema also defines metadata fields for data validation, such as required entry values, accepted formats, and character encoding. It aims to support structured data collection and ensure interoperability across platforms through consistent attribute naming and semantic annotations.
